translation: Nay, [man’s negative thinking on Resurrection is not right!] I do swear by the Day of Resurrection!
urdu: ہم کو روز قیامت کی قسم

translation: And, nay! I do swear by the self-reproaching soul [that man’s negative thinking on Resuuection is not right!]
notes: The Qur’aan speaks of 3 kinds of souls: one, prone to evil [[12:53]], two, self-reproaching [this Verse], and three, satisfied and at peace [[89:27]]. Allah Almighty swears here by the self-reproaching soul, thus giving importance to this stage in the development of human character. When a man starts listening to that inner voice of his own conscience, he has surely shown some progress from being utterly prone to evil. The development culminates in the soul being satisfied and at peace [ nafsu almutma-innah ] shunning all evil.
urdu: اور نفس لوامہ کی (کہ سب لوگ اٹھا کر) کھڑے کئے جائیں گے

translation: Does man think that We shall not be able to collect his bones?
urdu: کیا انسان یہ خیال کرتا ہے کہ ہم اس کی (بکھری ہوئی) ہڈیاں اکٹھی نہیں کریں گے؟

arabic: بَلَىٰ قَٰدِرِينَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّسَوِّيَ بَنَانَهُۥ
transliteration: Bala qadireena AAala an nusawwiya bananahu
translation: Yes, We have the power to restore [even] his fingertips.
notes: The design of fingertip ridges is unique to every human being: finger impressions of no two human beings are the same! This fact is made use of in forensic investigations and as a sure identity of any person. And this fact speaks volumes about the superhuman Ingenuity of the Creator. In this Verse, Allah Almighty draws our attention to that amazing fact and tells us that He can recreate even this unique feature of an individual human being when He resurrects him in the Hereafter.
urdu: ضرور کریں گے (اور) ہم اس بات پر قادر ہیں کہ اس کی پور پور درست کردیں

translation: But , man desires to go on doing evil in what lies ahead of him [in worldly life].
notes: Use of this word ‘but’ here is significant. It is not for want of cogent reasons that man would not believe in the Hereafter; the need for it – the inevitability of it – is ingrained in the very nature of man! But he suppresses this inner call of his conscience; for, otherwise, he would not be able to lead a profligate life, the Satan has lured him into.
urdu: مگر انسان چاہتا ہے کہ آگے کو خود سری کرتا جائے

translation: He asks, “When will the Day of Resurrection be?”
urdu: پوچھتا ہے کہ قیامت کا دن کب ہوگا؟

translation: So when the sight is dazzled,
urdu: جب آنکھیں چندھیا جائیں

translation: And the moon disappears,
urdu: اور چاند گہنا جائے

translation: And the sun and the moon are joined.
urdu: اور سورج اور چاند جمع کردیئے جائیں

translation: Man will ask that Day, “Where is the exit?”
urdu: اس دن انسان کہے گا کہ (اب) کہاں بھاگ جاؤں؟

translation: No! No escape!
urdu: بےشک کہیں پناہ نہیں

translation: With your Lord that day is the journey’s end!
urdu: اس روز پروردگار ہی کے پاس ٹھکانا ہے

arabic: يُنَبَّؤُاْ ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُ يَوۡمَئِذِۭ بِمَا قَدَّمَ وَأَخَّرَ
transliteration: Yunabbao alinsanu yawmaithin bima qaddama waakhkhara
translation: Man will be informed that Day what he had sent ahead and what he had left behind [undone].
notes: A non-believer, for example, would be informed that what he had sent ahead for that other world was nothing but utter doom for himself; and that what he had left behind undone would have secured for him an ever-lasting place in Paradise.
urdu: اس دن انسان کو جو (عمل) اس نے آگے بھیجے اور پیچھے چھوڑے ہوں گے سب بتا دیئے جائیں گے

translation: Man would rather be a witness against himself
urdu: بلکہ انسان آپ اپنا گواہ ہے

translation: Even though he may put up excuses.
urdu: اگرچہ عذر ومعذرت کرتا رہے

arabic: لَا تُحَرِّكۡ بِهِۦ لِسَانَكَ لِتَعۡجَلَ بِهِۦٓ
transliteration: La tuharrik bihi lisanaka litaAAjala bihi
translation: Move not [O Prophet] your tongue with it [revelation] to make haste with it.
notes: In the context of Verses, immediately following, it becomes apparent that the Prophet (peace on him), in the initial stages, was anxious that he would not forget, or make a mistake in remembering, what was being revealed to him. He would therefore try to utter words of the revelation even before it was completed. Hence the divine Advise to him in the Verses below!
urdu: اور (اے محمدﷺ) وحی کے پڑھنے کے لئے اپنی زبان نہ چلایا کرو کہ اس کو جلد یاد کرلو

translation: Its collection and recital does indeed rest on Us.
urdu: اس کا جمع کرنا اور پڑھانا ہمارے ذمے ہے

translation: So when We recite it, attentively follow its recitation.
urdu: جب ہم وحی پڑھا کریں تو تم (اس کو سنا کرو اور) پھر اسی طرح پڑھا کرو

translation: Then, indeed, it is for Us to make its meaning clear.
urdu: پھر اس (کے معانی) کا بیان بھی ہمارے ذمے ہے

translation: Nay, you [mankind] would rather love the fleeting present,
urdu: مگر (لوگو) تم دنیا کو دوست رکھتے ہو

translation: And neglect the Hereafter!
urdu: اور آخرت کو ترک کئے دیتے ہو

translation: Faces that day will be bright,
urdu: اس روز بہت سے منہ رونق دار ہوں گے

translation: Looking at their Lord expectantly.
urdu: اور) اپنے پروردگار کے محو دیدار ہوں گے

translation: And faces that day will be despondent,
urdu: اور بہت سے منہ اس دن اداس ہوں گے

translation: Expecting a great disaster to befall them.
urdu: خیال کریں گے کہ ان پر مصیبت واقع ہونے کو ہے

translation: Behold! When in pangs of death,
urdu: دیکھو جب جان گلے تک پہنچ جائے

translation: And question raised, “Any magician [who could save him]?”
urdu: اور لوگ کہنے لگیں (اس وقت) کون جھاڑ پھونک کرنے والا ہے

translation: And he thought it was the time for parting,
urdu: اور اس (جان بلب) نے سمجھا کہ اب سب سے جدائی ہے

translation: And the leg was put close to the [other] leg,
urdu: اور پنڈلی سے پنڈلی لپٹ جائے

translation: To your Lord was the direction that day.
urdu: اس دن تجھ کو اپنے پروردگار کی طرف چلنا ہے

translation: And neither did he stand by the truth nor did he pray,
urdu: تو اس (ناعاقبت) اندیش نے نہ تو (کلام خدا) کی تصدیق کی نہ نماز پڑھی

translation: And, on the other hand, he denied the Truth and turned his face away,
urdu: بلکہ جھٹلایا اور منہ پھیر لیا

translation: And then he went back to his people boasting [telling them how ‘bravely’ he denied the Truth]!
urdu: پھر اپنے گھر والوں کے پاس اکڑتا ہوا چل دیا

translation: [The Truth is drawing] nearer and nearer to you [O dying man],
urdu: افسوس ہے تجھ پر پھر افسوس ہے

translation: To repeat, [the Truth is drawing] nearer and nearer to you [O dying man]!
notes: When his heart stops beating, a man is technically dead. But from personal accounts of persons who had been declared technically dead and then resuscitated to life, the mind remains active for some time after the heart stops beating. That is because – for some seconds at least – mind cells continue to receive oxygen and nourishment from blood already pumped there. During those few seconds, the dying man gets partially aware of activities going on around him. And during those few seconds, he is also made aware of the Truth about life after death.
urdu: پھر افسوس ہے تجھ پر پھر افسوس ہے

translation: Does man think that he will be left to fend for himself?
notes: Most persons today (early part of 21 century) do not believe in any divinely revealed Book like the Qur’aan or the Torah, sent down for guidance of mankind. They are votaries of Darwin’s theory of evolution, and think that even if there be a God, He has left man, after creating him, to fend for himself!
urdu: کیا انسان خیال کرتا ہے کہ یوں ہی چھوڑ دیا جائے گا؟

arabic: أَلَمۡ يَكُ نُطۡفَةٗ مِّن مَّنِيّٖ يُمۡنَىٰ
transliteration: Alam yaku nutfatan min manayyin yumna
translation: Was he not a tiny ovum fertilised by sperm emitted?
urdu: کیا وہ منی کا جو رحم میں ڈالی جاتی ہے ایک قطرہ نہ تھا؟

translation: Then he became a leech-like clinging clot of curdled blood; and He created him and set his shape right.
urdu: پھر لوتھڑا ہوا پھر (خدا نے) اس کو بنایا پھر (اس کے اعضا کو) درست کیا

translation: Then He made from him pairs: male and female.
urdu: پھر اس کی دو قسمیں بنائیں (ایک) مرد اور (ایک) عورت

arabic: أَلَيۡسَ ذَٰلِكَ بِقَٰدِرٍ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يُحۡـِۧيَ ٱلۡمَوۡتَىٰ
transliteration: Alaysa thalika biqadirin AAala an yuhyiya almawta
translation: Is not That One [the Creator] powerful enough to give life to the dead!?
urdu: کیا اس خالق کو اس بات پر قدرت نہیں کہ مردوں کو جلا اُٹھائے؟
